prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, indistinct frame. Only faint vertical color bands are visible along the left side, and there is a visible “Classic Commercial Archives” watermark near the lower left, with another watermark across the lower middle.

The scene then cuts to a brightly lit interior. A blonde woman is seated at a table or vanity, facing the camera. She rests her head on one hand and appears to be speaking or posing. She has long, feathered hair and wears a shiny green blouse. Behind her is a window with daylight and greenery visible outside. There are decorative flowers near the window and on the right side of the frame. On the table in front of her are several objects, including a white cylindrical item and other small containers or reflective items that are not clearly identifiable.

The final frame shows a product display on a reflective surface. Two rectangular plastic bottles with black caps stand side by side. Their labels feature a photo of the same blonde woman. The left bottle is labeled “SHAMPOO,” and the right bottle is labeled “CREME RINSE CONDITIONER.” Additional smaller label text is present but not fully legible in the frame. A large cluster of pale flowers sits to the right of the bottles. The archival watermark remains visible over the image.
